title: chiogenes hispidula subsp. japonica
intro: Chiogenes hispidula subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese rose or mountain rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to Japan, characterized by its pink to purple flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Chiogenes hispidula subsp. japonica are said to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and wound-healing effects, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the skin to harness its medicinal properties.
